infobox.title: Whispering Woods
infobox.imageCaption: The canopy of the Whispering Woods at dusk

lead:
The **Whispering Woods** are an ancient and vast forest occupying the west-central region of [[Aethelgard]], spanning approximately 650,000 square kilometres. They are widely regarded as the oldest living entity on the continent — many scholars of the [[Lumina Collective]] believe the forest itself is semi-sentient, communicating through the subtle manipulation of sound and wind.

The forest is the ancestral home of the **Verdant Weavers**, practitioners of [[Life-Threads]] magic, who serve as its guardians and interpreters. Despite centuries of exploration by all four major factions, significant portions of the interior remain uncharted — cartographers consistently report that paths shift and landmarks move between expeditions.

content.content:
The Whispering Woods sustain a biological diversity unmatched anywhere in [[Aethelgard]]. Over 14,000 plant species have been catalogued, along with approximately 3,200 animal species — of which an estimated 800 have no equivalent outside the forest.

The forest's most striking feature is the **Canopy Network**: an interconnected system of massive, ancient trees whose root systems extend kilometres underground and are believed to exchange nutrients, information, and possibly emotional states through [[Life-Threads]] conduits. Verdant Weavers call these ancient trees the **Elders** and regard communication with them as the highest form of their craft.

content.alt: The inner canopy of the Whispering Woods
content.caption: The rarely photographed inner canopy, where aetheric light filters through ancient boughs

content.title: Navigation Warning
content.content: Travellers without a Verdant Weaver guide should not penetrate more than 20 km into the forest interior. The fog corridors shift unpredictably, magnetic compasses become unreliable within 50 km of the central nexus, and aetheric navigation devices have been known to malfunction entirely.

The **Verdant Weavers** are the human and near-human population of the Whispering Woods, numbering approximately 340,000. They live in harmony with the forest, building their settlements in the canopy and hollows of Elder trees rather than clearing ground.

Verdant Weavers do not have a centralised government in the conventional sense. Decisions are made by consensus among **Thread-Elders** — senior practitioners of [[Life-Threads]] magic who have spent decades in dialogue with the forest itself. Their society appears egalitarian from the outside, but carries complex hierarchies of spiritual seniority invisible to outsiders.

items.caption: A typical Verdant Weaver canopy village
A Thread-Elder communion ceremony at a forest nexus
The only maintained road through the western forest margin

content.content:
The Whispering Woods occupy a contested legal status in [[Aethelgard]]'s international framework. The **Green Charter** (1205 AE), signed at the end of the Verdant Wars, nominally recognises the forest as sovereign territory of the Verdant Weavers. However, the [[Ironclad Dominion]] considers the Charter non-binding as they did not sign it, and has conducted periodic logging and mining operations along the forest's eastern margin.

The **Canopy Reclamation Movement** (active since 1890 AE) is a Verdant Weaver political force that advocates for expanding the protected zone and prosecuting those who damage Elder trees — actions that Weavers class as murder rather than property destruction.
When you cut an Elder tree, you do not fell timber. You silence a voice that has spoken for a thousand years.
content.author: Thread-Elder Sylvara Rootwhisper, address to the Continental Assembly, 1897 AE

rows.Outcome: Ongoing; road permitted under restricted terms
